reason: Place reusable Git canonical-root logic in an Electron-free service/lib module and keep handlers thin.
Handle expected non-Git, timeout, and remote protocol failures without silent success-shaped corruption.
Follow existing backend/remote API request and response patterns for the batch canonical-root endpoint.
Keep Git/process work in the main or remote backend and expose only typed data to the renderer.
Extend project/alias shapes consistently across preload and renderer boundaries.
Maintain explicit, reusable TypeScript types across local and remote grouping paths.
Trace canonical project identity through resolver, API/IPC, backend bucketing, renderer state, and persisted aliases.
Use verified Git common-dir semantics, public comparable implementations, and documented edge-case handling.
